# Code statistics per variant

<!-- Generated by tools/code-stats.mjs (via scripts/code-stats.sh / .ps1). Do not edit by hand; rerun the tool. -->

One product, the same contract, implemented across many stacks. Files come
from `git ls-files` (tracked files only — no generated output, lockfiles git
ignores, or vendored caches; hidden dotfiles and dot-directories such as
`.yarn/` are excluded); line counts and languages come from
[`tokei`](https://github.com/XAMPPRocky/tokei), split by role:

| Role | What it counts |
|---|---|
| **Src** (app) | Implementation source, runtime/behavioural config, and `*.sql` migrations (`application.yml`, `nginx.conf`, `routes`, `server.xml`, ...) |
| **Test** | Test source (under `test/ tests/ __tests__/ spec/`, or named `*.test.*`, `*.spec.*`, `*Test.*`, `*Spec.*`, `*_test.go`, `test_*.py`) |
| **Cfg** (infra) | Build, dependency, tooling and container config (`build.gradle`, `pom.xml`, `*.csproj`, `Cargo.toml`, `go.mod`, `package.json`, `Dockerfile`, ...) |
| **Docs** | Prose (`*.md`, `LICENSE`, ...) |

**Src / Test / Cfg** are tokei `code` lines (standard LOC). **Docs** is prose
lines (`code + comments`) — Markdown has no `code` lines. **Test/Src** is
test-code / src-code. **Dockerfile** is Dockerfile `code` lines. Raw LOC is a
shape/verbosity signal, not a measure of effort or quality; Rust `#[cfg(test)]`
tests cannot be split by path, so they count as Src for `rust-axum`. Regenerate
with `./scripts/code-stats.sh --write docs/CODE-STATS.md` (or the `.ps1` flavor).
